# Tutorial Questions: Module 2 {-}

Note: * Indicates **Expanded** Competencies

## Question 1: Describing Distributions

(a) Construct a stem-and-leaf display of the following data:

2.73, 2.79, 2.85, 2.85, 2.53, 2.37, 2.61, 2.67, 2.78, 2.65, 2.84, 2.69, 2.72, 2.63, 2.68

Drag the **leaves** into the correct **stem**.

```{=html}
<!-- Leaf pool -->
<div id="leaf-pool">
<span class="leaf" id="leaf-1">3</span>
<span class="leaf" id="leaf-2">9</span>
<span class="leaf" id="leaf-3">5</span>
<span class="leaf" id="leaf-4">5</span>
<span class="leaf" id="leaf-5">3</span>
<span class="leaf" id="leaf-6">7</span>
<span class="leaf" id="leaf-7">1</span>
<span class="leaf" id="leaf-8">7</span>
<span class="leaf" id="leaf-9">8</span>
<span class="leaf" id="leaf-10">5</span>
<span class="leaf" id="leaf-11">4</span>
<span class="leaf" id="leaf-12">9</span>
<span class="leaf" id="leaf-13">2</span>
<span class="leaf" id="leaf-14">3</span>
<span class="leaf" id="leaf-15">8</span>
</div>

<!-- Stems -->
<div class="stem" data-stem="2.3">2.3 | </div>
<div class="stem" data-stem="2.5">2.5 | </div>
<div class="stem" data-stem="2.6">2.6 | </div>
<div class="stem" data-stem="2.7">2.7 | </div>
<div class="stem" data-stem="2.8">2.8 | </div>

<br>

<button onclick="checkAnswer()">Check Answer</button>
<button onclick="resetActivity()">Reset</button>
<button onclick="showHint()">Hint</button>

<p id="hint" style="display:none">
<strong>Hint:</strong> The stem is the first two digits.<br>
Example: <code>2.73 → stem 2.7, leaf 3</code>.

</p>

<p id="result" style="font-weight:bold;"></p>

<style>

#leaf-pool{
margin-bottom:15px;
}

.leaf{
display:inline-block;
padding:6px 10px;
margin:4px;
background:#e6f0ff;
border:1px solid #2a7ae2;
border-radius:4px;
cursor:grab;
}

.stem{
margin-top:10px;
padding:10px;
min-height:40px;
border:2px dashed #aaa;
}

</style>

<script>
let dragged = null;
const STORAGE_KEY = "stem_leaf_state";

// ---------- DRAG SETUP ----------
document.querySelectorAll('.leaf').forEach(el => {
  el.setAttribute("draggable","true");

  el.addEventListener("dragstart", e => {
    dragged = e.target;
    e.dataTransfer.setData("text/plain", ""); // Firefox
  });
});

document.querySelectorAll('.stem, #leaf-pool').forEach(container => {
  container.addEventListener("dragover", e => e.preventDefault());

  container.addEventListener("drop", e => {
    e.preventDefault();
    container.appendChild(dragged);
    saveState();
  });
});

// ---------- PERSISTENCE ----------
function saveState() {
  const state = {};

  document.querySelectorAll(".leaf").forEach(leaf => {
    state[leaf.id] = leaf.parentElement.id || leaf.parentElement.dataset.stem;
  });

  localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, JSON.stringify(state));
}

function restoreState() {
  const state = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem(STORAGE_KEY));
  if (!state) return;

  Object.entries(state).forEach(([leafId, containerId]) => {
    const leaf = document.getElementById(leafId);
    if (!leaf) return;

    if (containerId === "leaf-pool") {
      document.getElementById("leaf-pool").appendChild(leaf);
    } else {
      const stem = document.querySelector(`.stem[data-stem="${containerId}"]`);
      if (stem) stem.appendChild(leaf);
    }
  });
}

// Restore on load
restoreState();

// ---------- HINT ----------
function showHint(){
  document.getElementById("hint").style.display="block";
}

// ---------- CHECK ----------
function checkAnswer(){

  const correct = {
    "2.3":["7"],
    "2.5":["3"],
    "2.6":["1","3","5","7","8","9"],
    "2.7":["2","3","8","9"],
    "2.8":["4","5","5"]
  };

  let correctStem = 0;
  let ordered = 0;

  document.querySelectorAll(".stem").forEach(stem => {
    const label = stem.dataset.stem;
    const leaves = Array.from(stem.querySelectorAll(".leaf")).map(x => x.textContent);
    const sorted = [...leaves].sort();

    if (JSON.stringify(sorted) === JSON.stringify(correct[label])) {
      correctStem++;
      if (JSON.stringify(leaves) === JSON.stringify(sorted)) {
        ordered++;
      }
    }
  });

  if (correctStem === 5 && ordered === 5) {
    document.getElementById("result").innerHTML =
      "✔ Correct! Stems and leaves are correct and ordered.";
  }
  else if (correctStem === 5) {
    document.getElementById("result").innerHTML =
      "⚠ Half correct: stems are correct but leaves are not ordered.";
  }
  else {
    document.getElementById("result").innerHTML =
      "✘ Some stems are incorrect. Try again.";
  }
}

// ---------- RESET ----------
function resetActivity() {
  const pool = document.getElementById("leaf-pool");

  document.querySelectorAll(".leaf").forEach(leaf => pool.appendChild(leaf));

  localStorage.removeItem(STORAGE_KEY);
  document.getElementById("result").textContent = "";
  document.getElementById("hint").style.display = "none";
}
</script>
```

(b) Describe the distribution in terms of shape, centre, spread and outliers.

Select the most appropriate description.

``` {=html}
<hr>

<strong>(b) Describe the distribution</strong><br><br>

<strong>Shape</strong><br>
<select id="distB-shape">
  <option value="">Choose</option>
  <option value="symmetric">Symmetric</option>
  <option value="left">Skewed to the left</option>
  <option value="right">Skewed to the right</option>
  <option value="bimodal">Bimodal</option>
</select>

<hr>

<strong>Centre</strong><br>
<select id="distB-centre">
  <option value="">Choose</option>
  <option value="2.4">About 2.4</option>
  <option value="2.5">About 2.5</option>
  <option value="2.6-2.7">About 2.6–2.7</option>
  <option value="2.8">About 2.8</option>
</select>

<hr>

<strong>Spread</strong><br>
<select id="distB-spread">
  <option value="">Choose</option>
  <option value="full">2.37 to 2.85</option>
  <option value="253">2.53 to 2.85</option>
  <option value="261">2.61 to 2.84</option>
  <option value="237">2.37 to 2.72</option>
</select>

<hr>

<strong>Outliers</strong><br>
<select id="distB-outlier">
  <option value="">Choose</option>
  <option value="none">No unusual observations</option>
  <option value="237">2.37 may be a possible outlier</option>
  <option value="285">2.85 is an outlier</option>
  <option value="253">2.53 is an outlier</option>
</select>

<hr>

<button onclick="distB_check()">Check Answer</button>
<button onclick="distB_reset()">Reset</button>
<button onclick="distB_toggleHint()">Hint</button>

<p id="distB-result" style="font-weight:bold; margin-top:10px;"></p>

<p id="distB-hint" style="display:none;">
<strong>Hint:</strong>
Look at the direction of the tail, where most values cluster,
the full range of the data, and whether any value sits apart from the rest.
</p>

<script>
/* ---------- CONFIG ---------- */
const distB_STORAGE_KEY = "distB_distribution_state";

const distB_correct = {
  shape: "left",
  centre: "2.6-2.7",
  spread: "full",
  outlier: "237"
};

const distB_fields = ["shape", "centre", "spread", "outlier"];

/* ---------- SAVE / RESTORE ---------- */
function distB_saveState() {
  const state = {};
  distB_fields.forEach(key => {
    state[key] = document.getElementById("distB-" + key).value;
  });
  localStorage.setItem(distB_STORAGE_KEY, JSON.stringify(state));
}

function distB_restoreState() {
  const state = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem(distB_STORAGE_KEY));
  if (!state) return;

  distB_fields.forEach(key => {
    document.getElementById("distB-" + key).value = state[key];
  });
}

/* Restore on load */
distB_restoreState();

/* Save on change */
distB_fields.forEach(key => {
  document
    .getElementById("distB-" + key)
    .addEventListener("change", distB_saveState);
});

/* ---------- CHECK ---------- */
function distB_check() {
  let score = 0;

  distB_fields.forEach(key => {
    const sel = document.getElementById("distB-" + key);
    if (sel.value === distB_correct[key]) {
      sel.style.backgroundColor = "#c8f7c5"; // green
      score++;
    } else {
      sel.style.backgroundColor = "#f7c5c5"; // red
    }
  });

  const out = document.getElementById("distB-result");

  if (score === 4) {
    out.textContent =
      "✔ Correct. The distribution is left‑skewed, centred around 2.6–2.7, " +
      "with values from 2.37 to 2.85. The value 2.37 may be a possible outlier.";
  } else if (score >= 2) {
    out.textContent =
      "⚠ Partially correct. Review the skew, centre, and possible outliers.";
  } else {
    out.textContent =
      "✘ Not quite. Look carefully at the tail direction and overall spread.";
  }
}

/* ---------- RESET ---------- */
function distB_reset() {
  distB_fields.forEach(key => {
    const sel = document.getElementById("distB-" + key);
    sel.value = "";
    sel.style.backgroundColor = "";
  });

  localStorage.removeItem(distB_STORAGE_KEY);
  document.getElementById("distB-result").textContent = "";
  document.getElementById("distB-hint").style.display = "none";
}

/* ---------- HINT ---------- */
function distB_toggleHint() {
  const h = document.getElementById("distB-hint");
  h.style.display = (h.style.display === "none") ? "block" : "none";
}
</script>
```

## Question 2: Assignment Results

The following represent the assignment results (out of 100) for a class of 20 statistics students:

85, 38, 92, 93, 100, 88, 78, 79, 96, 81, 62, 39, 77, 78, 83, 91, 90, 85, 99, 97

```{r hist-assignment, fig.cap="Histogram of assignment results (n = 20) (\\#fig:hist-assignment)", echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)

# Data
scores <- c(85, 38, 92, 93, 100, 88, 78, 79, 96, 81, 62, 39, 77, 78, 83, 91, 90, 85, 99, 97)

# Histogram
p <- ggplot(data.frame(scores), aes(x = scores)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 10, fill = "#2a7ae2", color = "white", boundary = 0) +
  labs(title = "Histogram of Assignment Results (n=20)",
       x = "Assignment Results (out of 100)",
       y = "Frequency") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12)
  )

# Interactive
ggplotly(p)
```


For this data and Figure \@ref(fig:hist-assignment) complete the following questions:

(a) Describe the distribution of Assignment Results in terms of shape, centre, spread and outliers (including how many student results could be considered to be outliers).

<!-- =========================
Q2 (a) Describe the distribution
========================== -->

<!-- Shape -->
<p><strong>Shape:</strong></p>
<select id="q2-shape">
  <option value="">Choose</option>
  <option>Symmetric</option>
  <option>Skewed left</option>
  <option>Skewed right</option>
  <option>Bimodal</option>
</select>
<div>
  <button class="q2-btn" onclick="q2_hintA('shape')">Hint</button>
  <button class="q2-btn" onclick="q2_checkA('shape')">Check</button>
  <button class="q2-btn" onclick="q2_resetA('shape')">Reset</button>
</div>
<p id="q2-fb-shape" class="q2-feedback" aria-live="polite"></p>

<!-- Centre -->
<p style="margin-top:10px;"><strong>Centre:</strong></p>
<select id="q2-centre">
  <option value="">Choose</option>
  <option>70–79</option>
  <option>80–89</option>
  <option>90–99</option>
  <option>60–69</option>
</select>
<div>
  <button class="q2-btn" onclick="q2_hintA('centre')">Hint</button>
  <button class="q2-btn" onclick="q2_checkA('centre')">Check</button>
  <button class="q2-btn" onclick="q2_resetA('centre')">Reset</button>
</div>
<p id="q2-fb-centre" class="q2-feedback" aria-live="polite"></p>

<!-- Spread -->
<p style="margin-top:10px;"><strong>Spread:</strong></p>
<select id="q2-spread">
  <option value="">Choose</option>
  <option>38–100</option>
  <option>62–100</option>
  <option>70–95</option>
  <option>75–100</option>
</select>
<div>
  <button class="q2-btn" onclick="q2_hintA('spread')">Hint</button>
  <button class="q2-btn" onclick="q2_checkA('spread')">Check</button>
  <button class="q2-btn" onclick="q2_resetA('spread')">Reset</button>
</div>
<p id="q2-fb-spread" class="q2-feedback" aria-live="polite"></p>

<!-- Outliers -->
<p style="margin-top:10px;"><strong>Outliers:</strong></p>
<select id="q2-outlier">
  <option value="">Choose</option>
  <option>None</option>
  <option>1 student</option>
  <option>2 students</option>
  <option>More than 2</option>
</select>
<div>
  <button class="q2-btn" onclick="q2_hintA('outlier')">Hint</button>
  <button class="q2-btn" onclick="q2_checkA('outlier')">Check</button>
  <button class="q2-btn" onclick="q2_resetA('outlier')">Reset</button>
</div>
<p id="q2-fb-outlier" class="q2-feedback" aria-live="polite"></p>

<hr>

<!-- =========================
Q2 (b) Recommended centre & spread
========================== -->
<p class="h5">(b) What centre and spread would you use to describe this distribution? Why?</p>
<input type="text" id="q2-centrespread" placeholder="Type your answer here (e.g., median & IQR)" style="width:100%; max-width:420px;">
<div>
  <button class="q2-btn" onclick="q2_hintB()">Hint</button>
  <button class="q2-btn" onclick="q2_checkB()">Check</button>
  <button class="q2-btn" onclick="q2_resetB()">Reset</button>
</div>
<p id="q2-fb-b" class="q2-feedback" aria-live="polite"></p>

<hr>

<!-- =========================
Q2 (c) Mean
========================== -->
<p class="h5">(c) Using a computer or calculator, calculate the mean of the data set.</p>
<input type="number" step="0.01" id="q2-mean" placeholder="Enter mean" style="width:200px;">
<div>
  <button class="q2-btn" onclick="q2_hintC()">Hint</button>
  <button class="q2-btn" onclick="q2_checkC()">Check</button>
  <button class="q2-btn" onclick="q2_resetC()">Reset</button>
</div>
<p id="q2-fb-c" class="q2-feedback" aria-live="polite"></p>

<hr>

<!-- =========================
Q2 (d) Standard deviation
========================== -->
<p class="h5">(d) Using a computer or calculator, calculate the standard deviation of the data set.</p>
<input type="number" step="0.01" id="q2-sd" placeholder="Enter SD" style="width:200px;">
<div>
  <button class="q2-btn" onclick="q2_hintD()">Hint</button>
  <button class="q2-btn" onclick="q2_checkD()">Check</button>
  <button class="q2-btn" onclick="q2_resetD()">Reset</button>
</div>
<p id="q2-fb-d" class="q2-feedback" aria-live="polite"></p>

<hr>

<!-- =========================
Q2 (e) Median
========================== -->
<p class="h5">(e) Calculate the median of the data set (using both software and by hand).</p>
<input type="number" step="0.01" id="q2-median" placeholder="Enter median" style="width:200px;">
<div>
  <button class="q2-btn" onclick="q2_hintE()">Hint</button>
  <button class="q2-btn" onclick="q2_checkE()">Check</button>
  <button class="q2-btn" onclick="q2_resetE()">Reset</button>
</div>
<p id="q2-fb-e" class="q2-feedback" aria-live="polite"></p>

<hr>

<!-- =========================
Q2 (f) Explain mean vs median difference
========================== -->
<p class="h5">(f) Why are the mean and median different/similar?</p>
<textarea id="q2-meanmedian" placeholder="Explain here" rows="3" style="width:100%;"></textarea>
<div>
  <button class="q2-btn" onclick="q2_hintF()">Hint</button>
  <button class="q2-btn" onclick="q2_checkF()">Check</button>
  <button class="q2-btn" onclick="q2_resetF()">Reset</button>
</div>
<p id="q2-fb-f" class="q2-feedback" aria-live="polite"></p>

<!-- =========================
Styling for correct/incorrect
========================== -->
<style>
.q2-correct { background-color:#c8f7c5 !important; }
.q2-incorrect { background-color:#f7c5c5 !important; }
.q2-feedback {
  font-size: 0.9em;
  font-weight: normal;
  margin-top: 6px;
  color: #333;
}
.q2-btn { margin-right:6px; margin-top:6px; }
</style>

<!-- =========================
Logic
========================== -->
<script>
/* --------- Config --------- */
const q2cfg = {
  correct: {
    shape: "Skewed left",
    centre: "80–89",
    spread: "62–100",
    outlier: "2 students",
    mean: 81.55,
    sd: 17.33,
    median: 85
  },
  tol: 0.10 // numeric tolerance
};

/* --------- Helpers --------- */
function q2_setState(el, ok) {
  el.classList.remove('q2-correct','q2-incorrect');
  if (ok === true) el.classList.add('q2-correct');
  if (ok === false) el.classList.add('q2-incorrect');
}
function q2_clearState(el) {
  el.classList.remove('q2-correct','q2-incorrect');
}
function q2_setText(id, html) {
  document.getElementById(id).innerHTML = html || "";
}

/* ===== (a) per-subcontrol: shape/centre/spread/outlier ===== */
function q2_checkA(which) {
  const id = "q2-" + which;
  const el = document.getElementById(id);
  const val = el.value;
  const isCorrect = val === q2cfg.correct[which];
  q2_setState(el, isCorrect);
  const fb = isCorrect ? "✔ Correct." : "✘ Not quite. Try the hint.";
  q2_setText("q2-fb-" + which, fb);
}

function q2_hintA(which) {
  let msg = "";
  if (which === "shape") {
    msg = "Look at the tail: a few low marks (38 & 39) extend the left tail.";
  } else if (which === "centre") {
    msg = "Most observations lie in the 80s; the middle of the data is around there.";
  } else if (which === "spread") {
    msg = "Consider the range that captures the bulk of the data, not just the extreme values.";
  } else if (which === "outlier") {
    msg = "The two lowest scores (38 & 39) are likely outliers.";
  }
  q2_setText("q2-fb-" + which, msg);
}

function q2_resetA(which) {
  const id = "q2-" + which;
  const el = document.getElementById(id);
  el.value = "";
  q2_clearState(el);
  q2_setText("q2-fb-" + which, "");
}

/* ===== (b) centre & spread free text ===== */
function q2_checkB() {
  const el = document.getElementById("q2-centrespread");
  const txt = (el.value || "").toLowerCase();
  const ok = txt.includes("median") && (txt.includes("iqr") || txt.includes("interquartile"));
  q2_setState(el, ok);
  q2_setText("q2-fb-b", ok ? "✔ Correct: use the median & IQR for skewed data." :
                              "✘ Try again. For skewed data, use a robust centre and spread.");
}
function q2_hintB() {
  q2_setText("q2-fb-b", "Skewed distributions are best summarised by the median (centre) and IQR (spread).");
}
function q2_resetB() {
  const el = document.getElementById("q2-centrespread");
  el.value = "";
  q2_clearState(el);
  q2_setText("q2-fb-b", "");
}

/* ===== (c) mean ===== */
function q2_checkC() {
  const el = document.getElementById("q2-mean");
  const val = parseFloat(el.value);
  const ok = isFinite(val) && Math.abs(val - q2cfg.correct.mean) <= q2cfg.tol;
  q2_setState(el, ok);
  q2_setText("q2-fb-c", ok ? "✔ Correct (≈ 81.55)." : "✘ Not quite. Check your sum and divide by n = 20.");
}
function q2_hintC() {
  q2_setText("q2-fb-c", "Mean = (sum of all 20 scores) ÷ 20.");
}
function q2_resetC() {
  const el = document.getElementById("q2-mean");
  el.value = "";
  q2_clearState(el);
  q2_setText("q2-fb-c", "");
}

/* ===== (d) standard deviation ===== */
function q2_checkD() {
  const el = document.getElementById("q2-sd");
  const val = parseFloat(el.value);
  const ok = isFinite(val) && Math.abs(val - q2cfg.correct.sd) <= q2cfg.tol;
  q2_setState(el, ok);
  q2_setText("q2-fb-d", ok ? "✔ Correct (≈ 17.33)." :
                              "✘ Not quite. Use the sample SD and mind rounding.");
}
function q2_hintD() {
  q2_setText("q2-fb-d", "Use a calculator or statistical software and make sure you are calculating the sample standard deviation.");
}
function q2_resetD() {
  const el = document.getElementById("q2-sd");
  el.value = "";
  q2_clearState(el);
  q2_setText("q2-fb-d", "");
}

/* ===== (e) median ===== */
function q2_checkE() {
  const el = document.getElementById("q2-median");
  const val = parseFloat(el.value);
  const ok = isFinite(val) && Math.abs(val - q2cfg.correct.median) <= q2cfg.tol;
  q2_setState(el, ok);
  q2_setText("q2-fb-e", ok ? "✔ Correct (85)." : "✘ Not quite. Sort the 20 values and average the 10th & 11th.");
}
function q2_hintE() {
  q2_setText("q2-fb-e", "There are 20 observations, so the median is the average of the 10th and 11th values after ordering the data");
}
function q2_resetE() {
  const el = document.getElementById("q2-median");
  el.value = "";
  q2_clearState(el);
  q2_setText("q2-fb-e", "");
}

/* ===== (f) explanation ===== */
function q2_checkF() {
  const el = document.getElementById("q2-meanmedian");
  const txt = (el.value || "").toLowerCase();

  // Require key ideas: skew/outliers + effect on mean vs median robustness
  const hasSkew = txt.includes("skew");
  const hasOut = txt.includes("outlier") || txt.includes("38") || txt.includes("39");
  const talksMean = txt.includes("mean");
  const talksMedian = txt.includes("median");
  const pulls = txt.includes("pull") || txt.includes("drag") || txt.includes("lower") || txt.includes("influenc");
  const robust = txt.includes("robust") || txt.includes("resistant");

  const ok = (hasSkew || hasOut) && talksMean && talksMedian && (pulls || robust);

  q2_setState(el, ok);
  q2_setText("q2-fb-f",
    ok ? "✔ Good explanation." :
         "✘ Try to mention skew/outliers pull the mean, while the median is more robust."
  );
}
function q2_hintF() {
  q2_setText("q2-fb-f",
    "The two low outliers (38, 39) create left skew which pulls the mean down. " +
    "The median** is more robust to outliers."
  );
}
function q2_resetF() {
  const el = document.getElementById("q2-meanmedian");
  el.value = "";
  q2_clearState(el);
  q2_setText("q2-fb-f", "");
}
</script>

<!--
Q2(g)
-->
(g) Reproduce these results (graph and descriptive statistics) using a statistical software package. Tick each item below that you sucessfully reproduced.

<form id="qg-check">

  <label>
    <input type="checkbox" data-correct="true">
    I produced a histogram that matches the overall shape of the distribution
  </label><br>

  <label>
    <input type="checkbox" data-correct="true">
    My histogram shows a longer tail on the lower values (left skew)
  </label><br>

  <label>
    <input type="checkbox" data-correct="true">
    I calculated the mean using software
  </label><br>

  <label>
    <input type="checkbox" data-correct="true">
    I calculated the median using software
  </label><br>

  <label>
    <input type="checkbox" data-correct="true">
    I calculated the sample standard deviation using software
  </label><br>

  <label>
    <input type="checkbox" data-correct="true">
    I identified the same minimum and maximum values as shown above
  </label><br>

  <label>
    <input type="checkbox" data-correct="true">
    I can see two low values that may be considered potential outliers
  </label>

</form>

<div style="margin-top:12px;">
  <button onclick="qg_showHint()">Hint (Excel / JASP)</button>
  <button onclick="qg_check()">Check</button>
  <button onclick="qg_reset()">Reset</button>
</div>

<p id="qg-hint" class="qg-hint" style="display:none;"></p>
<p id="qg-feedback" class="qg-feedback"></p>

<style>
.qg-feedback {
  margin-top: 8px;
  font-size: 0.95em;
}

.qg-hint {
  margin-top: 8px;
  font-size: 0.9em;
  color: #333;
}

.qg-correct {
  outline: 3px solid #7bc67b;
}

.qg-incorrect {
  outline: 3px solid #e07b7b;
}
</style>

<script>
function qg_showHint(){
  document.getElementById("qg-hint").innerHTML =
    "Excel: Enter the data in a column. Use Insert → Charts → Histogram. " +
    "To obtain statistics, use Data → Data Analysis → Descriptive Statistics (or formulas such as AVERAGE, MEDIAN, STDEV.S). " +
    "JASP: Go to Descriptives, move the variable into the analysis box, and tick Histogram and Descriptive Statistics.";
  document.getElementById("qg-hint").style.display = "block";
}

function qg_check(){
  let allChecked = true;

  document.querySelectorAll("#qg-check input[type='checkbox']").forEach(box => {
    box.classList.remove("qg-correct","qg-incorrect");

    if(box.checked){
      box.classList.add("qg-correct");
    } else {
      box.classList.add("qg-incorrect");
      allChecked = false;
    }
  });

  document.getElementById("qg-feedback").innerHTML =
    allChecked
      ? "✔ Good — you have successfully reproduced the graph and descriptive statistics."
      : "⚠ Some items are not yet ticked. Review your output and check again.";
}

function qg_reset(){
  document.querySelectorAll("#qg-check input[type='checkbox']").forEach(box => {
    box.checked = false;
    box.classList.remove("qg-correct","qg-incorrect");
  });
  document.getElementById("qg-feedback").innerHTML = "";
  document.getElementById("qg-hint").style.display = "none";
}
</script>


(h) What 5 numbers make up the five-number summary?
(i) *Calculate Q1 and Q3 of the data set by hand and then fill in the numbers of
the five-number summary.
(j) *Calculate the IQR of the data set by hand.



## Further Practice from the Textbook

Answers to these questions are located in the back of the textbook. If you require further explanation, please ask in the Module Content Discussion Forum on the Study-
Desk.

• Questions 3.25, 4.31, 4.33 12.21, 12.47* (De Veaux, Velleman & Bock, (4th edition))

• Questions 2.53, 4.31, 4.33, 11.21,11.47* (De Veaux, Velleman & Bock, (5th edition))

## Textbook Readings 
For extra clarification on materials in this module, see: De Veaux, Velleman
& Bock, (4th edition), Chapters 3, 4, and 12 (De Veaux, Velleman
& Bock, (5th edition), Chapters 2.2-2.5, 4, and 11).


# Module 2 Checklist {-}
